Therapeutics

Direct disease associations, biomarker-linked drugs, OSMF narrative-review natural agents, merged ranking, and clinical trial / literature evidence (top agents)

From opensourcemed.info/chronic-disease — Integrative interventions for improving outcomes in depression: a narrative review. Candidates for further study, not approved treatments.

DrugTypePhaseEvidence ScoreTrials & literatureSourcesLinks
AGOMELATINE Small molecule Approved Strong 100
8 trials 13 Cochrane 23 Meta-analysis 49 Trial pubs 42 RCT
View evidence

Registry trials

  • NCT01156415 — Open-label Long Term (52 Weeks) Safety and Tolerability of Agomelatine Sublingual Tablets in Major Depressive Disorder (MDD) (TERMINATED) PHASE3
  • NCT04589143 — A Double-blind, Placebo-controlled Study of Antidepressant Augmentation With Agomelatine (COMPLETED) PHASE4
  • NCT01488071 — A Study of Vortioxetine (Lu AA21004) in Comparison to Agomelatine in Adults Suffering From Major Depression With Inadequate Response to Previous Medication (COMPLETED) PHASE3
  • NCT01531309 — Pharmacokinetics of AGO178 in Participants With Liver Impairment (COMPLETED) PHASE1
  • NCT04181567 — Comparison of the Efficacy, Safety, and Relapse of ECT to ECT Plus Agomelatine for Depressed Patients (COMPLETED) PHASE4
  • NCT01110889 — Efficacy, Safety and Tolerability of Agomelatine Sublingual Tablets in the Treatment of Major Depressive Disorder (COMPLETED) PHASE3
  • NCT00411099 — Efficacy, Safety and Tolerability of Agomelatine in the Treatment of Major Depressive Disorder (COMPLETED) PHASE3
  • NCT01731899 — Agomelatine in Depressed Patients With Fibromyalgia (COMPLETED)

Published literature

  • Cochrane review Side effect profile and comparative tolerability of newer generation antidepressants in the acute treatment of major depressive disorder in children and adolescents: protocol for a systematic review and network meta-analysis (BMJ open, 2025)
  • Cochrane review Comparative efficacy of antidepressant medication for adolescent depression: a network meta-analysis and systematic review (BMC psychiatry, 2025)
  • Cochrane review A comprehensive review and meta-analysis of neurological side effects related to second-generation antidepressants in individuals with major depressive disorder (Behavioural brain research, 2023)
  • Cochrane review Antidepressants for the treatment of adults with major depressive disorder in the maintenance phase: a systematic review and network meta-analysis (Molecular psychiatry, 2023)
  • Cochrane review Agomelatine: An Astounding Sui-generis Antidepressant? (Current molecular pharmacology, 2022)
  • Meta-analysis Comparative gastrointestinal effects of antidepressants for the acute treatment of adults with major depressive disorder: a network and dose‒response meta-analysis (Translational psychiatry, 2025)
  • Meta-analysis Efficacy and Acceptability of Licensed and Off-Label Pharmacological Interventions for Insomnia in Patients With Severe Mental Illness: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis of Randomised Trials (Acta psychiatrica Scandinavica, 2025)
  • Meta-analysis Efficacy and Safety of Agomelatine in Depressed Patients with Diabetes: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis (International journal of molecular sciences, 2024)
  • Meta-analysis Effect of antidepressants on ejaculation dysfunction in patients with depression and anxiety: A systematic review and network meta-analysis (Andrology, 2025)
  • Clinical trial publication Agomelatine as adjunctive therapy with SSRIs or SNRIs for major depressive disorder: a multicentre, double-blind, randomized, placebo-controlled trial (BMC medicine, 2025)
  • Clinical trial publication Agomelatine in pediatric patients with moderate to severe major depressive disorder: an open-label extension study (European child & adolescent psychiatry, 2025)
  • Clinical trial publication Changes of Serum C-Reactive Protein Level in Patients With Depressive Disorders After Treatment With Agomelatine Combined With Aerobic Exercise and Its Significance (Clinical neuropharmacology, 2023)

CT.gov search · Cochrane · PubMed

 Open Targets, ChEMBL ChEMBL Open Targets
AMPHETAMINE Small molecule Approved Strong 100
8 trials 2 Trial pubs 2 RCT
View evidence

Registry trials

  • NCT02058693 — Adjunctive Mixed Salts Amphetamine for Depressed Adults With Incomplete Response to Current Antidepressant Therapy (COMPLETED) PHASE4
  • NCT06967857 — Clinical Trial to Investigate the Safety and Efficacy of Two Dexamfetamine Sulfate Formulations in Adults With ADHD and Moderate to Severe Depression (RECRUITING) PHASE2
  • NCT00985725 — SPD489 in Adults With Persistent Executive Function Impairments (EFI) and Partial or Full Remission of Recurrent Major Depressive Disorder (COMPLETED) PHASE2
  • NCT00296686 — Sequential Tranylcypromine (TC), TC + Dextroamphetamine and TC + Triiodothyronine for Refractory Depression (TERMINATED) PHASE4
  • NCT01435759 — SPD489 in Combination With an Antidepressant in the Treatment of Adults With Major Depressive Disorder (COMPLETED) PHASE2
  • NCT01436162 — Efficacy and Safety Study of SPD489 in Combination With an Antidepressant in the Treatment of Adults With Major Depressive Disorder (COMPLETED) PHASE3
  • NCT01148979 — Lisdexamfetamine Dimesylate in Residual Symptoms and Cognitive Impairment in Major Depressive Disorder. (COMPLETED) PHASE4
  • NCT00905424 — Exploratory Study of SPD489 in Adults With Major Depressive Disorder (MDD) as Augmentation Therapy to an Antidepressant (COMPLETED) PHASE2

Published literature

  • Systematic review Navigating the challenges of substance use and psychopathology in depression, bipolar disorder, and schizophrenia (Comprehensive psychiatry, 2025)
  • Systematic review Amphetamine Use in the Elderly: A Systematic Review of the Literature (Current neuropharmacology, 2020)
  • Clinical trial publication Longitudinal study of impact of medication for opioid use disorder on Hamilton Depression Rating Scale (Journal of affective disorders, 2022)
  • Clinical trial publication Lisdexamfetamine dimesylate augmentation for adults with major depressive disorder and inadequate response to antidepressant monotherapy: Results from 2 phase 3, multicenter, randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled studies (Journal of affective disorders, 2016)

CT.gov search · Cochrane · PubMed

 Open Targets, ChEMBL ChEMBL Open Targets
ARIPIPRAZOLE Small molecule Approved Strong 100 — Open Targets, ChEMBL ChEMBL Open Targets
BREXPIPRAZOLE Small molecule Approved Strong 100
8 trials 6 Cochrane 15 Meta-analysis 27 Trial pubs 18 RCT
View evidence

Registry trials

  • NCT01360632 — Study of the Safety and Efficacy of Two Fixed Doses of OPC-34712 as Adjunctive Therapy in the Treatment of Adults With Major Depressive Disorder (the Polaris Trial) (COMPLETED) PHASE3
  • NCT01942785 — Study of Brexpiprazole as Adjunctive Treatment of Irritability in Patients With Major Depressive Disorder and an Inadequate Response to Antidepressant Therapy (COMPLETED) PHASE3
  • NCT03487198 — The Safety and Efficacy of Brexpiprazole as Adjunctive Therapy in the Treatment of Major Depressive Disorder (TERMINATED) PHASE3
  • NCT01838681 — Brexpiprazole as Adjunctive Treatment in Patients With Major Depressive Disorder With an Inadequate Response to Antidepressant Treatment (COMPLETED) PHASE3
  • NCT02212613 — Brexpiprazole (OPC 34712) Trial in the Treatment of Adults With Major Depressive Disorder and Irritability (WITHDRAWN) PHASE3
  • NCT04162522 — Canadian Biomarker Integration Network for Depression (CAN-BIND) - Validation Study (COMPLETED) PHASE3
  • NCT01360645 — Study of the Safety and Efficacy of Fixed Dose OPC-34712 as Adjunctive Therapy in the Treatment of Adults With Major Depressive Disorder (the Pyxis Trial) (COMPLETED) PHASE3
  • NCT05017311 — Optimized Predictive Treatment In Medications for Unipolar Major Depression (OPTIMUM-D) (RECRUITING) PHASE4

Published literature

  • Cochrane review Adjunctive Antipsychotics in Major Depressive Disorder: A Systematic Review and Network Meta-Analysis (JAMA psychiatry, 2026)
  • Cochrane review Comparative efficacy and safety of 4 atypical antipsychotics augmentation treatment for major depressive disorder in adults: A systematic review and network meta-analysis (Medicine, 2023)
  • Cochrane review Efficacy and Safety of Adjunctive Serotonin-Dopamine Activity Modulators in Major Depression: A Meta-Analysis of Randomized Controlled Trials (Journal of clinical pharmacology, 2022)
  • Cochrane review Efficacy and safety of brexpiprazole in acute management of psychiatric disorders: a meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials (International clinical psychopharmacology, 2020)
  • Cochrane review Medication-Induced Akathisia with Newly Approved Antipsychotics in Patients with a Severe Mental Illness: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis (CNS drugs, 2019)
  • Meta-analysis Comparison of brexpiprazole, aripiprazole, and placebo for Japanese major depressive disorder: A systematic review and network meta-analysis (Neuropsychopharmacology reports, 2024)
  • Meta-analysis Probiotics for adults with major depressive disorder compared with antidepressants: a systematic review and network meta-analysis (Nutrition reviews, 2025)
  • Meta-analysis Efficacy and acceptability of second-generation antipsychotics with antidepressants in unipolar depression augmentation: a systematic review and network meta-analysis (Psychological medicine, 2022)
  • Clinical trial publication Effects of Adjunctive Brexpiprazole in Major Depressive Disorder Analyzed by Baseline Anxiety Level: Post Hoc Analysis of a Phase 4 Study (Journal of clinical psychopharmacology, 2026)
  • Clinical trial publication Adjunctive brexpiprazole in patients with major depressive disorder who show minimal or partial response to antidepressant treatment: post hoc analysis of randomized controlled trials (The international journal of neuropsychopharmacology, 2025)
  • Clinical trial publication Patient-reported efficacy of adjunctive brexpiprazole in major depressive disorder in a phase 4, open-label, interventional study (Current medical research and opinion, 2025)
  • Clinical trial publication Adjunctive brexpiprazole in patients with unresolved symptoms of depression on antidepressant treatment who are early in the disease course: post hoc analysis of randomized controlled trials (The international journal of neuropsychopharmacology, 2025)

BUPROPION HYDROCHLORIDE Small molecule Approved Strong 100
8 trials 4 Trial pubs 3 RCT
View evidence

Registry trials

  • NCT00234195 — Wellbutrin XL, Major Depressive Disorder and Breast Cancer (COMPLETED) PHASE4
  • NCT01838876 — Long-term Safety and Tolerability of Cariprazine as an Adjunctive Treatment to Antidepressant Therapy in Patients With Major Depressive Disorder (COMPLETED) PHASE3
  • NCT07523048 — Dextromethorphan-Bupropion on Striatal Activity in Adults With Major Depressive Disorder (RECRUITING) PHASE2
  • NCT01369290 — Electroencephalography (EEG) Signal Processing (UNKNOWN)
  • NCT00590863 — Combining Medications to Enhance Depression Outcomes (COMPLETED) PHASE4
  • NCT00289523 — EEG Biomarkers for Predicting Response to Antidepressant Therapy (COMPLETED)
  • NCT06417437 — Non-invasive BCI and Application Verification for Depressed People (RECRUITING) PHASE4
  • NCT00093288 — Major Depressive Disorder In The Elderly (COMPLETED) PHASE3

Published literature

  • Clinical trial publication Efficacy and safety of bupropion hydrochloride extended-release versus escitalopram oxalate in Chinese patients with major depressive disorder: Results from a randomized, double-blind, non-inferiority trial (Journal of affective disorders, 2019)
  • Clinical trial publication An open-label, rater-blinded, 8-week trial of bupropion hydrochloride extended-release in patients with major depressive disorder with atypical features (Pharmacopsychiatry, 2013)
  • Clinical trial publication Selecting among second-step antidepressant medication monotherapies: predictive value of clinical, demographic, or first-step treatment features (Archives of general psychiatry, 2008)
  • Clinical trial publication Cost-effectiveness of evidence-based pharmacotherapy or cognitive behavior therapy compared with community referral for major depression in predominantly low-income minority women (Archives of general psychiatry, 2005)

CITALOPRAM Small molecule Approved Strong 100
8 trials 27 Cochrane 46 Meta-analysis 248 Trial pubs 167 RCT
View evidence

Registry trials

  • NCT01034995 — A Trial Evaluating the Efficacy and Tolerability of SSR125543 in Outpatients With Major Depressive Disorder (COMPLETED) PHASE2
  • NCT05002309 — Treatment Interrupts Depression Early (RECRUITING) PHASE2
  • NCT00166114 — Depression, Epinephrine, and Platelet Function (COMPLETED) PHASE4
  • NCT00464711 — Brain GABA Levels and Treatment Response in Major Depressive Disorder (COMPLETED) PHASE4
  • NCT01369095 — Efficacy and Safety of Fixed Doses of BMS 820836 in the Treatment of Patients With Treatment Resistant Major Depression (COMPLETED) PHASE2
  • NCT01198795 — Safety Study of Escitalopram in Children 7 to 11 Years of Age With Major Depressive Disorder (COMPLETED) PHASE4
  • NCT01312922 — Pipamperone/Citalopram (PNB01) Versus Citalopram (CIT) and Versus Pipamperone (PIP) in Major Depressive Disorder (MDD) (COMPLETED) PHASE3
  • NCT07478796 — TDM-Guided Treatment With SSRIs in Hospitalized Adults and Children (RECRUITING) PHASE4

Published literature

  • Cochrane review Side effect profile and comparative tolerability of newer generation antidepressants in the acute treatment of major depressive disorder in children and adolescents: protocol for a systematic review and network meta-analysis (BMJ open, 2025)
  • Cochrane review Effects of the selective serotonin reuptake inhibitors citalopram and escitalopram on glucolipid metabolism: a systematic review (Frontiers in endocrinology, 2025)
  • Cochrane review A Review of Transcranial Magnetic Stimulation and Transcranial Direct Current Stimulation Combined with Medication and Psychotherapy for Depression (Harvard review of psychiatry, 2024)
  • Cochrane review Escitalopram versus other antidepressive agents for major depressive disorder: a systematic review and meta-analysis (BMC psychiatry, 2023)
  • Cochrane review Efficacy and tolerability of antidepressant drugs in treatment of depression in children and adolescents: a network meta-analysis (Zhejiang da xue xue bao. Yi xue ban = Journal of Zhejiang University. Medical sciences, 2022)
  • Meta-analysis Probiotics for adults with major depressive disorder compared with antidepressants: a systematic review and network meta-analysis (Nutrition reviews, 2025)
  • Meta-analysis Antidepressants for the treatment of adults with major depressive disorder in the maintenance phase: a systematic review and network meta-analysis (Molecular psychiatry, 2023)
  • Meta-analysis New generation antidepressants for depression in children and adolescents: a network meta-analysis (The Cochrane database of systematic reviews, 2021)

Curcumin OSMF review Nutraceutical Preclinical Emerging 30
2 trials 2 Meta-analysis 9 Trial pubs 8 RCT
View evidence

Registry trials

  • NCT01750359 — Efficacy and Safety Curcumin in Depression (COMPLETED) PHASE4
  • NCT01022632 — Effect of Curcumin as Nutraceutical in Patients of Depression (COMPLETED) NA

Published literature

  • Meta-analysis Curcumin for depression: a meta-analysis (Critical reviews in food science and nutrition, 2020)
  • Meta-analysis The Role of Curcumin Administration in Patients with Major Depressive Disorder: Mini Meta-Analysis of Clinical Trials (Phytotherapy research : PTR, 2016)
  • Systematic review Curcumin for neuropsychiatric disorders: a review of in vitro, animal and human studies (Journal of psychopharmacology (Oxford, England), 2017)
  • Clinical trial publication Tetrahydrocurcumin for Major Depressive Disorder with Therapeutic Potential and Mechanistic Insights from Clinical and Preclinical Studies (Molecular neurobiology, 2025)
  • Clinical trial publication Add-on Treatment with Curcumin Has Antidepressive Effects in Thai Patients with Major Depression: Results of a Randomized Double-Blind Placebo-Controlled Study (Neurotoxicity research, 2018)
  • Clinical trial publication Efficacy of curcumin, and a saffron/curcumin combination for the treatment of major depression: A randomised, double-blind, placebo-controlled study (Journal of affective disorders, 2017)
  • Clinical trial publication Chronic Supplementation of Curcumin Enhances the Efficacy of Antidepressants in Major Depressive Disorder: A Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled Pilot Study (Journal of clinical psychopharmacology, 2015)
  • Clinical trial publication Curcumin and major depression: a randomised, double-blind, placebo-controlled trial investigating the potential of peripheral biomarkers to predict treatment response and antidepressant mechanisms of change (European neuropsychopharmacology : the journal of the European College of Neuropsychopharmacology, 2015)

  • NCT07521358 — Magnesium Supplementation With Fluoxetine for Cognitive Function in Depression (COMPLETED) NA
  • NCT01941043 — A Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled, Sequential Parallel Study of CERC-301 in the Adjunctive Treatment of Subjects With Severe Depression and Recent Active Suicidal Ideation Despite Antidepressant Treatment (COMPLETED) PHASE2
  • NCT04880460 — Effect of Magnesium Supplementation in Selective Serotonin Reuptake Inhibitors Treated Major Depressive Disorder Patients (COMPLETED) PHASE2

Psilocybin OSMF review Psychedelic Preclinical Emerging 30
8 trials 8 Cochrane 12 Meta-analysis 33 Trial pubs 25 RCT
View evidence

Registry trials

  • NCT05434156 — ELE-101 Safety & Tolerability Study in Healthy Participants and Patients With Depression (ACTIVE_NOT_RECRUITING) PHASE1, PHASE2
  • NCT06308653 — Psilocybin for Major Depressive Disorder (MDD) (ACTIVE_NOT_RECRUITING) PHASE3
  • NCT06624137 — Computer Game, Qualitative, and MEG/EEG Assessment of Serotonergic Psychedelics (RECRUITING)
  • NCT05381974 — The Effects of Psilocybin on Self-Focus and Self-Related Processing in Treatment Resistant MDD (WITHDRAWN) PHASE2
  • NCT05399498 — Psilocybin in Co-occuring Major Depressive Disorder and Borderline Personality Disorder (COMPLETED) PHASE2
  • NCT05259943 — Microdosing Psychedelics to Improve Mood (COMPLETED) PHASE2
  • NCT06141876 — Evaluation of Psilocybin-Assisted Psychotherapy in Treating Severe Depression in Patients With PTSD (UNKNOWN) PHASE2
  • NCT03181529 — Effects of Psilocybin in Major Depressive Disorder (COMPLETED) PHASE2

Published literature

  • Cochrane review Therapeutic effects of psilocybin in major depressive disorder: a systematic review and meta-analysis exploring dose effects (European archives of psychiatry and clinical neuroscience, 2026)
  • Cochrane review Efficacy and safety of psilocybin in the treatment of Major Depressive Disorder (MDD): A dose-response network meta-analysis of randomized placebo-controlled clinical trials (Psychiatry research, 2025)
  • Cochrane review Randomized Controlled Trials of Psilocybin-Assisted Therapy in the Treatment of Major Depressive Disorder: Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis (Acta psychiatrica Scandinavica, 2025)
  • Cochrane review Psilocybin for major depressive disorder: An updated systematic review and meta-analysis of randomized clinical trials (Journal of psychopharmacology (Oxford, England), 2026)
  • Cochrane review [Treatment-resistant depression. From classification to new therapies.] (Rivista di psichiatria, 2022)
  • Meta-analysis Psilocybin-assisted therapy for major depressive disorder: Perspective from meta-analysis (Journal of affective disorders, 2026)
  • Meta-analysis Methodological moderators of psilocybin-assisted therapy in depression: A systematic review and meta-analysis (Neuroscience and biobehavioral reviews, 2026)
  • Meta-analysis Breaking the chains of depression: A systematic review and meta-analysis of psilocybin therapy (Journal of affective disorders, 2026)
  • Meta-analysis Control Group Outcomes in Trials of Psilocybin, SSRIs, or Esketamine for Depression: A Meta-Analysis (JAMA network open, 2025)
  • Systematic review Temporal dynamics in neuroimaging as correlates of therapeutic response to psilocybin in major depressive disorder: A systematic review and critical appraisal (Journal of affective disorders, 2026)
  • Clinical trial publication Short-Term and Late-Term Effects of Psilocybin on Symptoms in Major Depression: A Randomized Clinical Trial (JAMA network open, 2026)
  • Clinical trial publication Evaluation of a facilitator training program in a randomized controlled trial of psilocybin treatment for depression (BMC medical education, 2026)

1 Meta-analysis 4 Trial pubs 4 RCT
View evidence

Published literature

  • Meta-analysis Saffron (Crocus sativus L.) and major depressive disorder: a meta-analysis of randomized clinical trials (Journal of integrative medicine, 2013)
  • Systematic review A systematic review of randomized controlled trials examining the effectiveness of saffron (Crocus sativus L.) on psychological and behavioral outcomes (Journal of integrative medicine, 2015)
  • Clinical trial publication Crocus sativus L. versus Citalopram in the Treatment of Major Depressive Disorder with Anxious Distress: A Double-Blind, Controlled Clinical Trial (Pharmacopsychiatry, 2017)
  • Clinical trial publication Crocin, the main active saffron constituent, as an adjunctive treatment in major depressive disorder: a randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled, pilot clinical trial (Journal of affective disorders, 2015)
  • Clinical trial publication Saffron for treatment of fluoxetine-induced sexual dysfunction in women: randomized double-blind placebo-controlled study (Human psychopharmacology, 2013)
  • Clinical trial publication Effect of saffron on fluoxetine-induced sexual impairment in men: randomized double-blind placebo-controlled trial (Psychopharmacology, 2012)

Vitamin D OSMF review Supplement Preclinical Emerging 30
7 trials 2 Cochrane 4 Meta-analysis 11 Trial pubs 10 RCT
View evidence

Registry trials

  • NCT04898725 — Effects of Vitamin D Supplementation on Depression and Inflammatory Markers (COMPLETED) NA
  • NCT01390662 — Can Vitamin D3 Supplementation Affect Treatment Outcomes in Patients With Depression (UNKNOWN) PHASE4
  • NCT05899660 — Impact of Omega 3 in Alcohol Use Disorder (RECRUITING) NA
  • NCT01932931 — Vitamin D, SSRIs and the Musculoskeletal System (COMPLETED) NA
  • NCT03754712 — Effect of Vitamin D Administration Along With SSRIs in Patients With Major Depressive Disorder (UNKNOWN) NA
  • NCT07579078 — Effect of Vitamin D Supplementation on Brain Waves in Female Major Depressive Disorder Patients With Hypovitaminosis D (ENROLLING_BY_INVITATION) NA
  • NCT00005119 — Altered Calcium and Vitamin D in PMDD or Severe PMS (COMPLETED) NA

Published literature

  • Cochrane review Efficacy and acceptability of vitamin D supplements for depressed patients: A systematic review and meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials (Nutrition (Burbank, Los Angeles County, Calif.), 2023)
  • Cochrane review Efficacy of vitamin D supplementation in major depression: A meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials (Journal of postgraduate medicine, 2019)
  • Meta-analysis Genome-wide interaction study with major depression identifies novel variants associated with cognitive function (Molecular psychiatry, 2022)
  • Meta-analysis The effect of vitamin D supplement on negative emotions: A systematic review and meta-analysis (Depression and anxiety, 2020)
  • Systematic review Efficacy of B-vitamins and vitamin D therapy in improving depressive and anxiety disorders: a systematic review of randomized controlled trials (Nutritional neuroscience, 2023)
  • Systematic review Nutrient and genetic biomarkers of nutraceutical treatment response in mood and psychotic disorders: a systematic review (Nutritional neuroscience, 2021)
  • Clinical trial publication Do probiotics modulate dietary intake? Pilot data from a randomized controlled sub-study of the ProBioHRV clinical trial in patients with depression and healthy controls (PloS one, 2026)
  • Clinical trial publication Effect of a six-month vitamin D supplementation on depressive symptoms in patients with major depressive episode (DepFuD): a double-blinded randomised controlled trial (Journal of affective disorders, 2026)
  • Clinical trial publication Vitamin D supplementation and incidence of major depressive disorder - A randomized clinical trial (Clinical nutrition (Edinburgh, Scotland), 2026)
  • Clinical trial publication The protective effect of vitamin D supplementation as adjunctive therapy to antidepressants on brain structural and functional connectivity of patients with major depressive disorder: a randomized controlled trial (Psychological medicine, 2024)
  • Clinical trial publication Effects of Vitamin D(3) and Marine Omega-3 Fatty Acids Supplementation on Indicated and Selective Prevention of Depression in Older Adults: Results From the Clinical Center Sub-Cohort of the VITamin D and OmegA-3 TriaL (VITAL) (The Journal of clinical psychiatry, 2023)

AGOMELATINE Small molecule Approved Strong 100
8 trials 13 Cochrane 23 Meta-analysis 49 Trial pubs 42 RCT
View evidence

Registry trials

  • NCT01156415 — Open-label Long Term (52 Weeks) Safety and Tolerability of Agomelatine Sublingual Tablets in Major Depressive Disorder (MDD) (TERMINATED) PHASE3
  • NCT04589143 — A Double-blind, Placebo-controlled Study of Antidepressant Augmentation With Agomelatine (COMPLETED) PHASE4
  • NCT01488071 — A Study of Vortioxetine (Lu AA21004) in Comparison to Agomelatine in Adults Suffering From Major Depression With Inadequate Response to Previous Medication (COMPLETED) PHASE3
  • NCT01531309 — Pharmacokinetics of AGO178 in Participants With Liver Impairment (COMPLETED) PHASE1
  • NCT04181567 — Comparison of the Efficacy, Safety, and Relapse of ECT to ECT Plus Agomelatine for Depressed Patients (COMPLETED) PHASE4
  • NCT01110889 — Efficacy, Safety and Tolerability of Agomelatine Sublingual Tablets in the Treatment of Major Depressive Disorder (COMPLETED) PHASE3
  • NCT00411099 — Efficacy, Safety and Tolerability of Agomelatine in the Treatment of Major Depressive Disorder (COMPLETED) PHASE3
  • NCT01731899 — Agomelatine in Depressed Patients With Fibromyalgia (COMPLETED)

Published literature

  • Cochrane review Side effect profile and comparative tolerability of newer generation antidepressants in the acute treatment of major depressive disorder in children and adolescents: protocol for a systematic review and network meta-analysis (BMJ open, 2025)
  • Cochrane review Comparative efficacy of antidepressant medication for adolescent depression: a network meta-analysis and systematic review (BMC psychiatry, 2025)
  • Cochrane review A comprehensive review and meta-analysis of neurological side effects related to second-generation antidepressants in individuals with major depressive disorder (Behavioural brain research, 2023)
  • Cochrane review Antidepressants for the treatment of adults with major depressive disorder in the maintenance phase: a systematic review and network meta-analysis (Molecular psychiatry, 2023)
  • Cochrane review Agomelatine: An Astounding Sui-generis Antidepressant? (Current molecular pharmacology, 2022)
  • Meta-analysis Comparative gastrointestinal effects of antidepressants for the acute treatment of adults with major depressive disorder: a network and dose‒response meta-analysis (Translational psychiatry, 2025)
  • Meta-analysis Efficacy and Acceptability of Licensed and Off-Label Pharmacological Interventions for Insomnia in Patients With Severe Mental Illness: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis of Randomised Trials (Acta psychiatrica Scandinavica, 2025)
  • Meta-analysis Efficacy and Safety of Agomelatine in Depressed Patients with Diabetes: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis (International journal of molecular sciences, 2024)
  • Meta-analysis Effect of antidepressants on ejaculation dysfunction in patients with depression and anxiety: A systematic review and network meta-analysis (Andrology, 2025)
  • Clinical trial publication Agomelatine as adjunctive therapy with SSRIs or SNRIs for major depressive disorder: a multicentre, double-blind, randomized, placebo-controlled trial (BMC medicine, 2025)
  • Clinical trial publication Agomelatine in pediatric patients with moderate to severe major depressive disorder: an open-label extension study (European child & adolescent psychiatry, 2025)
  • Clinical trial publication Changes of Serum C-Reactive Protein Level in Patients With Depressive Disorders After Treatment With Agomelatine Combined With Aerobic Exercise and Its Significance (Clinical neuropharmacology, 2023)

BREXPIPRAZOLE Small molecule Approved Strong 100
8 trials 6 Cochrane 15 Meta-analysis 27 Trial pubs 18 RCT
View evidence

Registry trials

  • NCT01360632 — Study of the Safety and Efficacy of Two Fixed Doses of OPC-34712 as Adjunctive Therapy in the Treatment of Adults With Major Depressive Disorder (the Polaris Trial) (COMPLETED) PHASE3

  • NCT01838681 — Brexpiprazole as Adjunctive Treatment in Patients With Major Depressive Disorder With an Inadequate Response to Antidepressant Treatment (COMPLETED) PHASE3
  • NCT02212613 — Brexpiprazole (OPC 34712) Trial in the Treatment of Adults With Major Depressive Disorder and Irritability (WITHDRAWN) PHASE3
  • NCT04162522 — Canadian Biomarker Integration Network for Depression (CAN-BIND) - Validation Study (COMPLETED) PHASE3
  • NCT01360645 — Study of the Safety and Efficacy of Fixed Dose OPC-34712 as Adjunctive Therapy in the Treatment of Adults With Major Depressive Disorder (the Pyxis Trial) (COMPLETED) PHASE3

  • Cochrane review Efficacy and Safety of Adjunctive Serotonin-Dopamine Activity Modulators in Major Depression: A Meta-Analysis of Randomized Controlled Trials (Journal of clinical pharmacology, 2022)
  • Cochrane review Efficacy and safety of brexpiprazole in acute management of psychiatric disorders: a meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials (International clinical psychopharmacology, 2020)

  • Meta-analysis Comparison of brexpiprazole, aripiprazole, and placebo for Japanese major depressive disorder: A systematic review and network meta-analysis (Neuropsychopharmacology reports, 2024)
  • Meta-analysis Probiotics for adults with major depressive disorder compared with antidepressants: a systematic review and network meta-analysis (Nutrition reviews, 2025)
  • Meta-analysis Efficacy and acceptability of second-generation antipsychotics with antidepressants in unipolar depression augmentation: a systematic review and network meta-analysis (Psychological medicine, 2022)
  • Clinical trial publication Effects of Adjunctive Brexpiprazole in Major Depressive Disorder Analyzed by Baseline Anxiety Level: Post Hoc Analysis of a Phase 4 Study (Journal of clinical psychopharmacology, 2026)
  • Clinical trial publication Adjunctive brexpiprazole in patients with major depressive disorder who show minimal or partial response to antidepressant treatment: post hoc analysis of randomized controlled trials (The international journal of neuropsychopharmacology, 2025)
  • Clinical trial publication Patient-reported efficacy of adjunctive brexpiprazole in major depressive disorder in a phase 4, open-label, interventional study (Current medical research and opinion, 2025)
  • Clinical trial publication Adjunctive brexpiprazole in patients with unresolved symptoms of depression on antidepressant treatment who are early in the disease course: post hoc analysis of randomized controlled trials (The international journal of neuropsychopharmacology, 2025)

  • Meta-analysis Comparative gastrointestinal effects of antidepressants for the acute treatment of adults with major depressive disorder: a network and dose‒response meta-analysis (Translational psychiatry, 2025)
  • Meta-analysis The risks of adverse events with mirtazapine for adults with major depressive disorder: a systematic review with meta-analysis and trial sequential analysis (BMC psychiatry, 2025)
  • Systematic review The Efficacy of Pharmacological Treatment of Depression in Anorexia Nervosa and Underweight Patients: A Systematic Review (European eating disorders review : the journal of the Eating Disorders Association, 2026)
  • Clinical trial publication Measurement-Based Care to Enhance Antidepressant Treatment Outcomes in Major Depressive Disorder: A Randomized Clinical Trial (JAMA network open, 2025)
  • Clinical trial publication Consistent differential effects of bupropion and mirtazapine in major depression (Journal of affective disorders, 2025)
  • Clinical trial publication Reduced urine pregnenolone concentration after clinical response in patients with depression: An open-label short-term prospective study (Psychoneuroendocrinology, 2023)

  • Cochrane review The effect of paroxetine on heart rate variability in patients with major depressive disorder: A systematic review and meta-analysis (Journal of affective disorders, 2024)

  • Meta-analysis Comparative gastrointestinal effects of antidepressants for the acute treatment of adults with major depressive disorder: a network and dose‒response meta-analysis (Translational psychiatry, 2025)

  • Meta-analysis Effect of antidepressants on ejaculation dysfunction in patients with depression and anxiety: A systematic review and network meta-analysis (Andrology, 2025)

  • Clinical trial publication Interpersonal sensitivity and response to selective serotonin reuptake inhibitors in patients with acute major depressive disorder (Journal of affective disorders, 2024)

Registry trials

  • NCT02058693 — Adjunctive Mixed Salts Amphetamine for Depressed Adults With Incomplete Response to Current Antidepressant Therapy (COMPLETED) PHASE4
  • NCT06967857 — Clinical Trial to Investigate the Safety and Efficacy of Two Dexamfetamine Sulfate Formulations in Adults With ADHD and Moderate to Severe Depression (RECRUITING) PHASE2
  • NCT00985725 — SPD489 in Adults With Persistent Executive Function Impairments (EFI) and Partial or Full Remission of Recurrent Major Depressive Disorder (COMPLETED) PHASE2
  • NCT00296686 — Sequential Tranylcypromine (TC), TC + Dextroamphetamine and TC + Triiodothyronine for Refractory Depression (TERMINATED) PHASE4
  • NCT01435759 — SPD489 in Combination With an Antidepressant in the Treatment of Adults With Major Depressive Disorder (COMPLETED) PHASE2
  • NCT01436162 — Efficacy and Safety Study of SPD489 in Combination With an Antidepressant in the Treatment of Adults With Major Depressive Disorder (COMPLETED) PHASE3
  • NCT01148979 — Lisdexamfetamine Dimesylate in Residual Symptoms and Cognitive Impairment in Major Depressive Disorder. (COMPLETED) PHASE4
  • NCT00905424 — Exploratory Study of SPD489 in Adults With Major Depressive Disorder (MDD) as Augmentation Therapy to an Antidepressant (COMPLETED) PHASE2

Published literature

  • Systematic review Navigating the challenges of substance use and psychopathology in depression, bipolar disorder, and schizophrenia (Comprehensive psychiatry, 2025)
  • Systematic review Amphetamine Use in the Elderly: A Systematic Review of the Literature (Current neuropharmacology, 2020)
  • Clinical trial publication Longitudinal study of impact of medication for opioid use disorder on Hamilton Depression Rating Scale (Journal of affective disorders, 2022)
  • Clinical trial publication Lisdexamfetamine dimesylate augmentation for adults with major depressive disorder and inadequate response to antidepressant monotherapy: Results from 2 phase 3, multicenter, randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled studies (Journal of affective disorders, 2016)

5 trials 6 Cochrane 8 Meta-analysis 20 Trial pubs 14 RCT
View evidence

Registry trials

  • NCT01305707 — Continuation Electroconvulsive Therapy (C-ECT) for Relapse Prevention in Major Depression (TERMINATED) PHASE4
  • NCT00944996 — Assessment of Pituitary Adenylate Cyclase Activating Polypeptide-Brain Derived Neurotrophic Factor (PACAP-BDNF) Signaling System Involvement in Etiology and Treatment of Major Depression (COMPLETED) NA
  • NCT01944657 — Supplemental Transcranial Magnetic Stimulation (TMS) vs. Standard Medication Monotherapy for Treating Major Depression: An Exploratory Field Study (WITHDRAWN) NA
  • NCT00704860 — Treatment-Resistant Depression, Hippocampus Atrophy and Serotonin Genetic Polymorphism (COMPLETED) PHASE4
  • NCT05952713 — Comparative Responses to 15 Different Antidepressants in Major Depressive Disorder (COMPLETED)

Published literature

  • Cochrane review Efficacy and dose-response relationships of antidepressants in the acute treatment of major depressive disorders: a systematic review and network meta-analysis (Chinese medical journal, 2025)
  • Cochrane review Tricyclic antidepressants versus 'active placebo', placebo or no intervention for adults with major depressive disorder: a protocol for a systematic review with meta-analysis and Trial Sequential Analysis (Systematic reviews, 2021)
  • Cochrane review Beneficial and harmful effects of antidepressants versus placebo, 'active placebo', or no intervention for adults with major depressive disorder: a protocol for a systematic review of published and unpublished data with meta-analyses and trial sequential analyses (Systematic reviews, 2021)
  • Cochrane review Pharmacological interventions targeting anhedonia in patients with major depressive disorder: A systematic review (Progress in neuro-psychopharmacology & biological psychiatry, 2019)
  • Cochrane review Comparative efficacy and acceptability of 21 antidepressant drugs for the acute treatment of adults with major depressive disorder: a systematic review and network meta-analysis (Lancet (London, England), 2018)
  • Meta-analysis Comparative gastrointestinal effects of antidepressants for the acute treatment of adults with major depressive disorder: a network and dose‒response meta-analysis (Translational psychiatry, 2025)
  • Meta-analysis Effect of antidepressants on ejaculation dysfunction in patients with depression and anxiety: A systematic review and network meta-analysis (Andrology, 2025)
  • Meta-analysis Comparative efficacy and tolerability of antidepressants for major depressive disorder in children and adolescents: a network meta-analysis (Lancet (London, England), 2016)
  • Clinical trial publication Effectiveness of Genotype-Specific Tricyclic Antidepressant Dosing in Patients With Major Depressive Disorder: A Randomized Clinical Trial (JAMA network open, 2023)
  • Clinical trial publication Is trazodone more effective than clomipramine in major depressed outpatients? A single-blind study with intravenous and oral administration (CNS spectrums, 2019)
  • Clinical trial publication Are antidepressants equally effective in the long-term treatment of major depressive disorder? (Human psychopharmacology, 2015)
  • Clinical trial publication The predictive validity of atypical neurovegetative depressive symptoms identified by the first principal component in the DUAG trial of moclobemide versus clomipramine (Journal of affective disorders, 2012)

Curcumin OSMF review Nutraceutical Preclinical Emerging 30
2 trials 2 Meta-analysis 9 Trial pubs 8 RCT
View evidence

Registry trials

  • NCT01750359 — Efficacy and Safety Curcumin in Depression (COMPLETED) PHASE4
  • NCT01022632 — Effect of Curcumin as Nutraceutical in Patients of Depression (COMPLETED) NA

Published literature

  • Meta-analysis Curcumin for depression: a meta-analysis (Critical reviews in food science and nutrition, 2020)
  • Meta-analysis The Role of Curcumin Administration in Patients with Major Depressive Disorder: Mini Meta-Analysis of Clinical Trials (Phytotherapy research : PTR, 2016)
  • Systematic review Curcumin for neuropsychiatric disorders: a review of in vitro, animal and human studies (Journal of psychopharmacology (Oxford, England), 2017)
  • Clinical trial publication Tetrahydrocurcumin for Major Depressive Disorder with Therapeutic Potential and Mechanistic Insights from Clinical and Preclinical Studies (Molecular neurobiology, 2025)
  • Clinical trial publication Add-on Treatment with Curcumin Has Antidepressive Effects in Thai Patients with Major Depression: Results of a Randomized Double-Blind Placebo-Controlled Study (Neurotoxicity research, 2018)
  • Clinical trial publication Efficacy of curcumin, and a saffron/curcumin combination for the treatment of major depression: A randomised, double-blind, placebo-controlled study (Journal of affective disorders, 2017)
  • Clinical trial publication Chronic Supplementation of Curcumin Enhances the Efficacy of Antidepressants in Major Depressive Disorder: A Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled Pilot Study (Journal of clinical psychopharmacology, 2015)
  • Clinical trial publication Curcumin and major depression: a randomised, double-blind, placebo-controlled trial investigating the potential of peripheral biomarkers to predict treatment response and antidepressant mechanisms of change (European neuropsychopharmacology : the journal of the European College of Neuropsychopharmacology, 2015)

Psilocybin OSMF review Psychedelic Preclinical Emerging 30
8 trials 8 Cochrane 12 Meta-analysis 33 Trial pubs 25 RCT
View evidence

Registry trials

  • NCT05434156 — ELE-101 Safety & Tolerability Study in Healthy Participants and Patients With Depression (ACTIVE_NOT_RECRUITING) PHASE1, PHASE2
  • NCT06308653 — Psilocybin for Major Depressive Disorder (MDD) (ACTIVE_NOT_RECRUITING) PHASE3
  • NCT06624137 — Computer Game, Qualitative, and MEG/EEG Assessment of Serotonergic Psychedelics (RECRUITING)
  • NCT05381974 — The Effects of Psilocybin on Self-Focus and Self-Related Processing in Treatment Resistant MDD (WITHDRAWN) PHASE2
  • NCT05399498 — Psilocybin in Co-occuring Major Depressive Disorder and Borderline Personality Disorder (COMPLETED) PHASE2
  • NCT05259943 — Microdosing Psychedelics to Improve Mood (COMPLETED) PHASE2
  • NCT06141876 — Evaluation of Psilocybin-Assisted Psychotherapy in Treating Severe Depression in Patients With PTSD (UNKNOWN) PHASE2
  • NCT03181529 — Effects of Psilocybin in Major Depressive Disorder (COMPLETED) PHASE2

Published literature

  • Cochrane review Therapeutic effects of psilocybin in major depressive disorder: a systematic review and meta-analysis exploring dose effects (European archives of psychiatry and clinical neuroscience, 2026)
  • Cochrane review Efficacy and safety of psilocybin in the treatment of Major Depressive Disorder (MDD): A dose-response network meta-analysis of randomized placebo-controlled clinical trials (Psychiatry research, 2025)
  • Cochrane review Randomized Controlled Trials of Psilocybin-Assisted Therapy in the Treatment of Major Depressive Disorder: Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis (Acta psychiatrica Scandinavica, 2025)
  • Cochrane review Psilocybin for major depressive disorder: An updated systematic review and meta-analysis of randomized clinical trials (Journal of psychopharmacology (Oxford, England), 2026)
  • Cochrane review [Treatment-resistant depression. From classification to new therapies.] (Rivista di psichiatria, 2022)
  • Meta-analysis Psilocybin-assisted therapy for major depressive disorder: Perspective from meta-analysis (Journal of affective disorders, 2026)
  • Meta-analysis Methodological moderators of psilocybin-assisted therapy in depression: A systematic review and meta-analysis (Neuroscience and biobehavioral reviews, 2026)
  • Meta-analysis Breaking the chains of depression: A systematic review and meta-analysis of psilocybin therapy (Journal of affective disorders, 2026)
  • Meta-analysis Control Group Outcomes in Trials of Psilocybin, SSRIs, or Esketamine for Depression: A Meta-Analysis (JAMA network open, 2025)
  • Systematic review Temporal dynamics in neuroimaging as correlates of therapeutic response to psilocybin in major depressive disorder: A systematic review and critical appraisal (Journal of affective disorders, 2026)
  • Clinical trial publication Short-Term and Late-Term Effects of Psilocybin on Symptoms in Major Depression: A Randomized Clinical Trial (JAMA network open, 2026)
  • Clinical trial publication Evaluation of a facilitator training program in a randomized controlled trial of psilocybin treatment for depression (BMC medical education, 2026)

  • NCT04898725 — Effects of Vitamin D Supplementation on Depression and Inflammatory Markers (COMPLETED) NA

  • NCT01932931 — Vitamin D, SSRIs and the Musculoskeletal System (COMPLETED) NA

Published literature

  • Cochrane review Efficacy and acceptability of vitamin D supplements for depressed patients: A systematic review and meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials (Nutrition (Burbank, Los Angeles County, Calif.), 2023)
  • Cochrane review Efficacy of vitamin D supplementation in major depression: A meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials (Journal of postgraduate medicine, 2019)
  • Meta-analysis Genome-wide interaction study with major depression identifies novel variants associated with cognitive function (Molecular psychiatry, 2022)
  • Meta-analysis The effect of vitamin D supplement on negative emotions: A systematic review and meta-analysis (Depression and anxiety, 2020)
  • Systematic review Efficacy of B-vitamins and vitamin D therapy in improving depressive and anxiety disorders: a systematic review of randomized controlled trials (Nutritional neuroscience, 2023)
  • Systematic review Nutrient and genetic biomarkers of nutraceutical treatment response in mood and psychotic disorders: a systematic review (Nutritional neuroscience, 2021)
  • Clinical trial publication Do probiotics modulate dietary intake? Pilot data from a randomized controlled sub-study of the ProBioHRV clinical trial in patients with depression and healthy controls (PloS one, 2026)
  • Clinical trial publication Effect of a six-month vitamin D supplementation on depressive symptoms in patients with major depressive episode (DepFuD): a double-blinded randomised controlled trial (Journal of affective disorders, 2026)
  • Clinical trial publication Vitamin D supplementation and incidence of major depressive disorder - A randomized clinical trial (Clinical nutrition (Edinburgh, Scotland), 2026)
  • Clinical trial publication The protective effect of vitamin D supplementation as adjunctive therapy to antidepressants on brain structural and functional connectivity of patients with major depressive disorder: a randomized controlled trial (Psychological medicine, 2024)
  • Clinical trial publication Effects of Vitamin D(3) and Marine Omega-3 Fatty Acids Supplementation on Indicated and Selective Prevention of Depression in Older Adults: Results From the Clinical Center Sub-Cohort of the VITamin D and OmegA-3 TriaL (VITAL) (The Journal of clinical psychiatry, 2023)

Alterations and therapeutics are aggregated from public databases (Open Targets, HPO, ChEMBL, DGIdb, and others). Natural agents are candidates from OSMF narrative reviews on opensourcemed.info/chronic-disease.html — not approved treatments. This page is for research orientation only — not medical advice.
